title: A-9 Vigilance interceptor
content:
{{Top|leg|title=A-9 ''Vigilance'' interceptor}} {{Otheruses|Vigilance}} {{Update|[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 22]]}} {{StarshipClass |image=[[File:A-9_NEGVV.png]] |name=A-9 ''Vigilance'' interceptor |designer= |manufacturer=[[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]]''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]''''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' |line=[[A-series interceptors]] |model=A-9 ''Vigilance'' interceptor |type=[[Starfighter/Legends|Starfighter]] |cost=*185,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] (New) *130,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] (Used)''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]'' |modifier= |sysmods= |length=7.4 meters[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 93|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 93]] |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed=1,300 [[Kilometer/Legends|km]]/[[Standard hour/Legends|h]] |maneuverability= |engine=[[KuatDriveSystems]] [[A-9x thrust engine]]s (2) |hyperdrive= |hdrange= |hdsystem=None |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen=None |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=Heavy [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] (2) |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew=[[Pilot/Legends|Pilot]] (1) |skeleton= |passengers= |capacity=55 kilograms |cargohandling= |consumables=1 day |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |availability= |role=Territorial defensive [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptor]] |firstuse=[[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]] |retired= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[Imperial Remnant]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] *[[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] *[[Five Worlds]] *[[Confederation]] }} {{Quote|A-9 ''Vigilance'' interceptors. Speedy little things.''"
"''Weak hulls. I used to crack 'em with my teeth and suck out the meat inside.|[[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]|''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]''}} The '''A-9 ''Vigilance'' interceptor''' was a short-range [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Dark Empire]] that began production in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]]. ==Characteristics== [[File:A9_schem.jpg|left|thumb|A-9 [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]].]] Simple cockpit controls made the A-9 an easy fighter to learn. While faster than the [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE Interceptor]], it had a weaker hull due to deliberate design choices made to reduce mass. Thanks to independent power generators, however, the weapons system was quite powerful for a ship of its size. The A-9 was armed with two laser cannons, mounted in a structure on the ventral side of the fuselage. One was slightly more powerful than the other. They were fire-linked and aimed forward in their standard configuration, but several modifications existed which allowed the gun to pivot 90 degrees down to perform strafing runs, as well as allowing the cannons to be independently targeted. These cannons were more powerful than those of a [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]]. ==History== Designed by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]], famous for its [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]], the A-9 was an attempt to enter the very profitable Imperial starfighter market. Though KDY was a major manufacturer of fighters for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Imperial market was largely dominated by [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]], makers of the [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]]. The A-9 was intended to fulfill the Imperial Starfighter Corps' desperate need for a faster, more maneuverable fighter to deal with the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wing]] [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptor]]. Released almost six years after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], shortly after the defeat of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], the vessel the Empire received did not fully meet these requirements. [[File:A9_Interceptor.jpg|thumb|left|250px|An A-9 ''Vigilance'' interceptor in flight]] In true Imperial fashion, the craft had no [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] or [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], requiring the craft to operate from a fixed base or [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. Like TIE series craft, A-9s were generally deployed in large numbers to compensate for their shortcomings and replaced as required. After [[Norval II]] rebelled against its governor [[Abam Tev]] and successfully removed its government, the New Republic gained access to the new A-9 interceptors. This proved to be a boon to the beleaguered New Republic military after the [[Second Battle of Coruscant|fall of Coruscant]], as the A-9, along with the new [[E-7 E-wing multi-role starfighter|E-wing]] fighter, helped to even the odds when [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] forces [[Battle of Calamari|attacked]] the ocean world of [[Dac/Legends|Dac]]. While it was never widely adopted because of its technical shortfalls, the A-9 continued to show up in [[Imperial Remnant]] fleets, as well as in New Republic/[[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] squadrons years after its introduction. It was often rumored that KDY was in the process of developing the [[A-10 interceptor]], which would address the problems with the A-9. In the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], a number of them were in the [[Corellian Defense Force/Legends|Corellian Defense Force]]. ==Behind the scenes== Starfighters very similar to the A-9 were seen in a captured Imperial hangar on [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]] in [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 22|''X-wing Rogue Squadron'' Issue 22]], but their class was not stated. They may be early prototypes of the A-9 or a similar-looking predecessor. [[A-6 Interceptor]]s were mentioned in ''[[Jedi Quest: The School of Fear]]'', but very little information was provided aside from the fact that they had [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] cannons and used [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droids]]. It was the predecessor of the A-9 interceptor. ==Appearances== *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 22|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 22]] {{C|Anachronistic appearance}} *[[Dark Empire 3|''Dark Empire'' 3]] {{1st}} *[[Dark Empire 6|''Dark Empire'' 6]] *[[Empire's End 1|''Empire's End'' 1]] *[[Empire's End 2|''Empire's End'' 2]] *''[[The New Jedi Order: Recovery]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' *''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]'' ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of A-9 Vigilance interceptors}} *''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' *''[[The Jedi Academy Sourcebook]]'' *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]'' *{{FactFile|13|KUA 1-4|Kuat Drive Yards}} *''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' *{{FactFile|93|VIG 1-2|A-9 Vigilance Interceptor}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Unknown Regions]]'' *{{DB|starship|a9vigilance|A9 Vigilance interceptor}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |cs=Interceptor A-9 Vigilance |de=A-9 Vigilance Abfangjäger |es=Interceptor de Patrulla A-9 }} [[Category:A-series interceptor models|A-09]] [[Category:Starship classes of the Galactic Alliance]] [[Category:Starship classes of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Starship classes of the New Republic]]
